challenges.title: Embedded Content
challenges.description: Create an embed element with src and type attributes to embed external content (e.g., a PDF or media file). Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<embed' in lower, 'Should contain an <embed> element'
assert 'src=' in lower, 'Embed should have a src attribute'
assert 'type=' in lower, 'Embed should have a type attribute'
hints: The <embed> element is a void element (no closing tag).
The type attribute specifies the MIME type, e.g. type="application/pdf".
You can also add width and height attributes.

challenges.title: Object Embed
challenges.description: Create both an embed element and an object element. The object element should contain at least one param child element and include fallback text. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create embed and object elements -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<embed' in lower, 'Should contain an <embed> element'
assert '<object' in lower, 'Should contain an <object> element'
assert '</object>' in lower, 'Should close the <object> element'
assert '<param' in lower, 'Object should contain a <param> element'
assert 'name=' in lower, 'Param should have a name attribute'
assert 'value=' in lower, 'Param should have a value attribute'
assert 'data=' in lower or 'type=' in lower, 'Object should have a data or type attribute'
hints: The <object> element uses data= instead of src= to specify the resource.
Each <param> element has name and value attributes.
Fallback content goes between the opening and closing <object> tags.
